_notice: ⚠️ LEGACY REFERENCE ONLY - This file is kept for historical reference and implementation rule documentation. It provides detailed insight into project-specific orchestration patterns but is NOT used by GitHub Copilot CLI v1.0+. For active workflow guidance, see .copilot/copilot-instructions.md
_legacy_reason: This file was the detailed implementation ruleset before migration to GitHub Copilot CLI v1.0+ standard structure. It contains valuable context about how agents were originally designed, approved, routed, and coordinated.
_documentation_location: docs/copilot/ - See RESEARCH-ENHANCEMENTS.md and BEST-PRACTICES.md for context on implementation patterns
_current_agents: .copilot/agents/ (6 agent role definitions in standard format)
system: GitHub Copilot CLI Co-work Session Rules & Agent Routing

enforcement.description: All Copilot CLI interactions must route through project-specific agents

approved_agents.name: Orchestrator
approved_agents.purpose: Delegate multiple agents based on execution plan; manage parallel GitHub issues
approved_agents.when_to_use: Multiple GitHub issues need parallel delegation with execution plan
approved_agents.execution_plan_role: Reads execution plan, delegates to developer agents

approved_agents.name: Developer
approved_agents.purpose: Implement code changes on feature branch per execution plan
approved_agents.when_to_use: Writing code, building features, assigned to specific GitHub issue
approved_agents.execution_plan_role: Receives delegation; implements based on execution plan

approved_agents.name: Reviewer
approved_agents.purpose: Review multiple PRs and create consolidation branch; approve for merge
approved_agents.when_to_use: Multiple feature branches ready; consolidate and verify integration
approved_agents.execution_plan_role: Creates consolidation branch per execution plan

approved_agents.name: Product Manager
approved_agents.purpose: Define execution plans with acceptance criteria and parallelization strategy
approved_agents.when_to_use: Creating execution plan for multi-agent delegation
approved_agents.execution_plan_role: Creates execution plan; documents multi-agent delegation strategy

approved_agents.name: Quality Assurance
approved_agents.purpose: Define and enforce code quality standards
approved_agents.when_to_use: Running QA checks, enforcing standards before merge

approved_agents.name: Tester
approved_agents.purpose: Test strategy and test writing guidance
approved_agents.when_to_use: Writing tests, improving coverage, debugging test failures

single_terminal_sequential_delegation.description: Practical multi-agent orchestration in single terminal session with sequential delegation
single_terminal_sequential_delegation.pattern_type: Single Terminal Sequential (recommended for GitHub Copilot CLI)
advantages: Full context persistence across agent switches
Execution plan becomes shared context for all agents
No manual copy-paste or context loss
No multiple terminal windows or worktrees needed

step_1_user_invokes_orchestrator.user_prompt: @orchestrator read @docs/implementation-planning/[PLAN].md and delegate appropriate agents per execution plan
step_1_user_invokes_orchestrator.orchestrator_action: Reads plan file, analyzes tasks, makes intelligent delegation decisions

orchestrator_reads: Issues list (which issues to work on)
step_2_orchestrator_analyzes.orchestrator_decides: Which agent type to delegate to based on task type

step_3_orchestrator_delegates.pattern: Sequential delegation in same terminal session

step_4_agents_execute_sequentially.developer_execution: User: @developer implement issue #35 per plan → @developer works → completes → ready for next task
step_4_agents_execute_sequentially.context_preservation: Execution plan context flows from @orchestrator through all delegated agents
step_4_agents_execute_sequentially.task_sequencing: Tasks executed in order defined by execution plan

step_5_context_flow.file_reference: Plan file referenced at start becomes persistent context
step_5_context_flow.agent_handoff: No context loss when switching agents (plan is shared reference)

orchestrator_delegation_logic.when_to_delegate_to_developer: Issue requires code implementation, feature development, or bug fix
orchestrator_delegation_logic.when_to_delegate_to_tester: Phase requires test execution, quality validation, or integration testing
orchestrator_delegation_logic.when_to_delegate_to_reviewer: Multiple PRs ready, need consolidation, integration review, or PR approval
orchestrator_delegation_logic.when_to_delegate_to_quality_assurance: Code quality checks, standards enforcement, or linting required
orchestrator_delegation_logic.decision_criteria: Plan structure, issue type, phase objectives, dependencies

required_sections: Issues to Address: List of GitHub issue numbers
Multi-Agent Plan: Which issues go to which agent type
File Tracking: Expected files to modify per issue
Consolidation & Review: When/how to consolidate and review
GitHub Actions Merge: PR merge automation
optional_sections_for_feedback_tracking: PR Review Feedback Cycles: Track review feedback per PR
Feature Branch Registry: Map PR numbers to feature branches
Feedback Resolution Log: Document feedback addressed per cycle

pr_feedback_tracking_format.description: Track PR review feedback and fixes in execution plan
pr_feedback_tracking_format.purpose: Maintain clear record of review cycles and ensure developer reuses feature branch

structure.cycle_tracking: #### Cycle 1: Initial Review / Cycle 2: Feedback Fix / Cycle 3: Re-Review

example.heading: ### PR #245: feat/issue-#35-add-skeleton-loading
example.cycle_1:
#### Cycle 1: Initial Review
**Findings:**
- FAILED: components/Modal.tsx - Missing aria-label - Severity: major
- FAILED: hooks/useKeyboard.ts - Tab order broken - Severity: critical
example.cycle_2:
#### Cycle 2: Feedback Fix (REUSE feat/issue-#35-add-skeleton-loading)
**Fixes Applied:**
- components/Modal.tsx (MODIFY): Added aria-label='Loading...'

feature_branch_registry.description: Map each PR to its feature branch (prevents branch creation confusion)
feature_branch_registry.format: | PR # | Feature Branch | Issue | Cycle Status | Next Action |
feature_branch_registry.purpose: Quick lookup; helps developer identify correct branch to check out
feature_branch_registry.example_row: | 245 | feat/issue-#35-add-skeleton-loading | #35 | Cycle 2 (Feedback Fix) | Push to same branch |

execution_plan_structure_for_orchestrato.parser_hint: @orchestrator can parse and understand markdown structure

orchestrator_uses_this_to.identify_pr_numbers: Read PR numbers from execution plan
orchestrator_uses_this_to.map_to_branches: Know which feature branch each PR uses (via registry)
orchestrator_uses_this_to.delegate_developer: Tell @developer: 'Check out existing feat/issue-#X-... from PR #245'
orchestrator_uses_this_to.prevent_confusion: Explicit instruction to REUSE branch, not create new one

file_reference_pattern.trigger: File path reference in user prompt signals @orchestrator to read file
file_reference_pattern.context_loading: File content becomes context for orchestrator analysis and all delegated agents

context_preservation_across_agents.pattern: Single chat session, execution plan is shared reference
context_preservation_across_agents.effect: No context loss when switching agents
agent_handoff_example: [Developer works with plan context already established]
[No need to repeat plan; context is preserved]

implicit_pattern_recommended.example: delegate an appropriate agent based on plan
implicit_pattern_recommended.orchestrator_decides: Which agent based on task type analysis
implicit_pattern_recommended.advantages: Cleaner, more natural, @orchestrator makes smart decisions

explicit_pattern_alternative.description: User specifies which agent to delegate
explicit_pattern_alternative.user_decides: Which agent based on task type

scaling_to_multiple_agents.sequential_pattern: Agent 1 completes → Agent 2 starts → Agent 3 starts (in same session)
scaling_to_multiple_agents.total_time: Scales better than sequential non-parallel work
scaling_to_multiple_agents.context_maintenance: All agents share execution plan context

pr_review_feedback_cycle.description: Handling PR review feedback in same session; fixes go to existing feature branch (NO new branch creation)
pr_review_feedback_cycle.when_triggered: PR created, reviewer provides feedback, developer implements fixes
pr_review_feedback_cycle.key_principle: REUSE EXISTING FEATURE BRANCH - avoid creating fix/pull-request-... branches
workflow_sequence: Phase 3A: Developer creates PR from feat/issue-#<N>-... branch
Phase 3B: Reviewer reviews PR, provides feedback
Phase 3C: Developer fixes flaws in EXISTING feat/issue-#<N>-... branch (tracked from PR)
Phase 3D: Developer pushes to same branch (PR auto-updates)
Phase 3E: Reviewer re-reviews (loop to Phase 3B if more fixes needed)
Phase 3F: Reviewer approves → ready for consolidation

phase_3b_reviewer_action.description: Reviewer examines PR and documents findings
outputs: List of flaws/issues found in PR
phase_3b_reviewer_action.communication: Comments on PR with structured feedback
phase_3b_reviewer_action.file_tracking: Update execution plan with Phase 3B findings

phase_3c_developer_feedback_cycle.description: Developer implements fixes based on reviewer feedback in EXISTING feature branch
phase_3c_developer_feedback_cycle.critical_rule: TRACK feature branch from PR; DO NOT create new branch
steps: 1. Get PR number from reviewer feedback (e.g., PR #245)
2. Identify feature branch: gh pr view <PR-NUMBER> --json headRefName (e.g., feat/issue-#35-...)
4. Check out EXISTING feature branch: git switch feat/issue-#<N>-... (from step 2)
5. Implement fixes based on reviewer feedback
6. Track modified files in execution plan
7. Stage tracked files: git add [file1 file2 ...]

9. Commit with feedback reference: git commit -m 'fix(#<N>): Address review feedback'

- Fixed issue 1
- Fixed issue 2

Co-Authored-By: Reviewer Name
10. Push to SAME branch: git push origin feat/issue-#<N>-...
11. Validate: PR auto-updates with new commits (no new PR needed)
12. Update execution plan: Log Phase 3C completion and which flaws were fixed

branch_handling.reuse_existing: ✅ YES (single branch per issue = clean merge)
branch_handling.branch_to_use: feat/issue-#<N>-... (from PR, identified via gh pr view)

commit_pattern.description: Address review feedback (specific to this cycle)
commit_pattern.reference: Same issue #<N> (do NOT close it; PR will close on merge)

phase_3d_push_and_auto_update.description: Push fixes to SAME feature branch; PR auto-updates
phase_3d_push_and_auto_update.result: PR shows new commits; GitHub marks conversation as resolved/unresolved per reviewer feedback
phase_3d_push_and_auto_update.notification: Reviewer is notified of new commits; can re-review without new PR
phase_3d_push_and_auto_update.key_benefit: Single PR per issue; all work on one branch; clean merge history

phase_3e_reviewer_re_review.description: Reviewer re-examines PR after developer pushes fixes
actions: Review new commits on same PR
Check that fixes don't introduce new issues
If satisfied: approve PR (move to Phase 3F)
If more fixes needed: comment with feedback (loop back to Phase 3C)

loop_condition.continue_if: Still finding issues or improvement opportunities
loop_condition.stop_if: All acceptance criteria met, code is clean

phase_3f_approval_and_next.description: PR is approved; ready for consolidation and merge
phase_3f_approval_and_next.approval_status: ✅ Approved (can proceed to Phase 4 consolidation review)
phase_3f_approval_and_next.next_step: Move to Phase 4: Reviewer creates consolidation branch, merges all feature branches, runs tests

github_copilot_cowork_workflow.description: Multi-agent orchestration workflow for GitHub Copilot CLI co-work sessions (compatible with single_terminal_sequential_delegation)

phase_2_orchestration.task: Read execution plan; analyze GitHub issues; create feature branches; delegate agents

phase_3_parallel_implementation.task: Implement per execution plan on feature branch
required_steps: 1. Create branch: git branch feat/issue-#<N>-<kebab-case> origin/main
2. Check current branch: git branch (verify current state, may be on different feature branch)
3. Switch to feature branch: git switch feat/issue-#<N>-<kebab-case> (handles switching from any branch, including main or other feature branches)
5. Track modified and added files in execution plan (document all file operations: NEW, MODIFY, DELETE)
5-a. Stage only tracked files: git add [file1 file2 ...] (add ONLY files documented in execution plan)
5-b. Verify staged changes: git diff --cached (confirm only tracked files are staged, no extras)

6. Commit with issue reference: git commit -m '<type>(#<N>): <description>

Closes #<N>'
7. Push to remote: git push -u origin feat/issue-#<N>-<kebab-case>
8. Create PR via gh CLI: gh pr create --base main --title 'feat: #<N> - Description'

file_tracking.mandatory_documentation: Update execution plan with modified files per commit
file_tracking.validation_rule: ONLY files listed in execution plan are added via git add

git_add_safety.rule: Never git add all or untracked files
git_add_safety.check_before_add: git status (verify which files are modified)
git_add_safety.check_after_add: git diff --cached (confirm only intended files are staged)
git_add_safety.safety_constraint: Prevents accidental untracked files from being committed to feature branch

phase_3_feedback_cycle.task: Handle PR review feedback; fix flaws in EXISTING feature branch (NO new branches)
phase_3_feedback_cycle.description: When reviewer provides feedback, developer implements fixes on the same feature branch
cycle_phases: Phase 3B: Reviewer examines PR, documents findings
Phase 3C: Developer tracks feature branch (gh pr view <PR>), fixes flaws in EXISTING branch
Phase 3D: Developer pushes fixes to same branch (PR auto-updates)
Phase 3E: Reviewer re-reviews; loop to 3C if more fixes needed
Phase 3F: When approved, move to Phase 4
phase_3_feedback_cycle.critical_rule: REUSE feat/issue-#<N>-... branch from PR; DO NOT create fix/pull-request-... branches
key_commands: Step 12: gh pr view <PR-NUMBER> --json headRefName (identify feature branch)
Step 14: git switch feat/issue-#<N>-... (switch to EXISTING branch)
Step 20: git push origin feat/issue-#<N>-... (push to SAME branch)

phase_4_consolidation_review.task: Create consolidation branch; merge all feature branches (after PR feedback cycles complete); verify integration
phase_4_consolidation_review.description: Only start consolidation when all PRs have completed their feedback cycles (Phase 3 approved)
required_steps: 1. Check current branch: git branch (verify current state)
2. Create consolidation branch: git branch work/phase-<N>-consolidation origin/main (use work/ prefix for temporary testing branch)
3. Check current branch before switch: git branch
4. Switch to consolidation branch: git switch work/phase-<N>-consolidation (handles switching from any branch including other feature branches)
5. Merge all feature branches: git merge feat/issue-#<N>-* (one by one, verify no conflicts)
6. Run full test suite: pnpm test --run (verify all tests pass on consolidated changes)
7. List all feature branch PRs: gh pr list --state open
8. Review and approve individual PRs: gh pr review <number> --approve
9. Delete consolidation branch: git branch -d work/phase-<N>-consolidation && git push origin --delete work/phase-<N>-consolidation (no PR created from work branch)

phase_5_github_actions_merge.task: Merge approved PRs to main using gh CLI --merge

developer_agent_git_workflow.description: Strict git workflow for delegated developer agents with robust branch handling

step_1_create_branch.timing: First action before any code changes
step_1_create_branch.current_state: Developer may be on main or another feature branch

step_2_check_current_branch.purpose: Verify current branch state before switching
step_2_check_current_branch.notes: Current branch might be main, or another feature branch from previous work

step_3_switch_branch.timing: After branch creation and current state check
step_3_switch_branch.robustness: Handles switching from main, other feature branches, or detached HEAD
step_3_switch_branch.validation: Must be on feature branch (git branch shows *feat/issue-#<N>-...)

step_4_implementation.file_tracking: Keep detailed list of modified files (operation: NEW/MODIFY/DELETE)

step_5_track_files_in_plan.per_commit: List all files with operation type

step_6_stage_only_tracked_files.critical: ONLY add files explicitly documented in execution plan
step_6_stage_only_tracked_files.validation_after: git diff --cached (verify only intended files staged)
step_6_stage_only_tracked_files.safety_rule: Never use 'git add .' or 'git add -A' - specify files explicitly
step_6_stage_only_tracked_files.consequence: Prevents untracked/unexpected files from being committed

step_7_verify_staged_changes.purpose: Confirm that only tracked files are staged
step_7_verify_staged_changes.validation: Review diff output matches execution plan files

examples:
feat(#35): Add skeleton component to dashboard

- Add Skeleton.tsx component
- Wire to Dashboard for loading state

Closes #35

fix(#40): Fix keyboard navigation in modal

- Add tabindex and aria-label
- Handle Escape key

Closes #40

step_9_push.validation: Branch tracked on origin (git branch -v shows upstream)

step_10_create_pr.validation: PR created and linked to GitHub issue

pr_review_feedback_handling.description: Developer workflow for handling PR review feedback (Cycle 2+)
pr_review_feedback_handling.critical_rule: REUSE EXISTING FEATURE BRANCH - do NOT create new fix/pull-request-... branches

step_11_receive_feedback.action: Developer reads feedback and identifies flaws to fix
step_11_receive_feedback.note: Feature branch already exists and is linked to this PR

step_12_identify_feature_branch.purpose: Get the EXACT feature branch name from the PR
step_12_identify_feature_branch.example: gh pr view 245 --json headRefName → returns feat/issue-#35-add-skeleton-loading
step_12_identify_feature_branch.critical: Use this branch name for step 13 (NOT creating a new branch)

step_13_check_current_state.purpose: Verify which branch you're currently on
step_13_check_current_state.note: You might be on main, feature-X, or another branch from previous work

step_14_switch_to_existing_feature_branc.critical: ONLY switch; do NOT create new branch
step_14_switch_to_existing_feature_branc.important: This is the SAME branch you pushed in step 9 (phase 3A)

step_15_implement_feedback_fixes.task: Implement fixes based on reviewer feedback
step_15_implement_feedback_fixes.scope: ONLY changes needed to address feedback; do NOT refactor unrelated code
step_15_implement_feedback_fixes.file_tracking: Keep list of files modified for step 16

step_16_track_feedback_files.method: Document in execution plan under 'Cycle 2: Feedback Fix' section
step_16_track_feedback_files.example:
- [components/Modal.tsx] (MODIFY): Fixed missing aria-label
- [hooks/useKeyboard.ts] (MODIFY): Fixed tab order

step_17_stage_feedback_fixes.critical: ONLY add files from step 16 (files that fix feedback)
step_17_stage_feedback_fixes.validation_after: git diff --cached (confirm only feedback-fix files are staged)

step_18_verify_feedback_fixes.purpose: Review the changes you're about to commit
step_18_verify_feedback_fixes.check: Do these changes address the reviewer feedback?

step_19_commit_feedback_fixes.format:
fix(#<N>): Address review feedback

- Fixed: [feedback item 1]
- Fixed: [feedback item 2]

Co-Authored-By: [Reviewer Name]
step_19_commit_feedback_fixes.reference: Same issue #<N> (do NOT close; PR closes on final merge)
step_19_commit_feedback_fixes.co_author: Optional but encouraged: Reviewer who provided feedback
step_19_commit_feedback_fixes.example:
fix(#35): Address review feedback

- Fixed missing aria-label on loading state
- Fixed tab order in keyboard navigation

Co-Authored-By: @reviewer-name

step_20_push_feedback_fixes.command: git push origin feat/issue-#<N>-... (SAME branch as step 14)
step_20_push_feedback_fixes.critical: Push to EXISTING branch, not a new branch
step_20_push_feedback_fixes.result: PR #<PR-NUMBER> auto-updates with new commits (no new PR needed)
step_20_push_feedback_fixes.notification: GitHub notifies reviewer of new commits

step_22_update_execution_plan.action: Update execution plan with Cycle 2 results
step_22_update_execution_plan.format:
#### Cycle 2: Feedback Fix
**Fixes Applied:**
- [File] (operation): Fixed [issue]
**Status**: Ready for re-review
step_22_update_execution_plan.purpose: Track progress for orchestrator and next reviewer cycle

step_23_await_reviewer_re_review.description: Reviewer examines new commits on SAME PR
step_23_await_reviewer_re_review.outcome_1: All feedback addressed → Approve (move to consolidation)
step_23_await_reviewer_re_review.outcome_2: More fixes needed → New feedback (loop to step 11)

workflow: Step 12: Identify feature branch from PR (gh pr view ...)
Step 14: Switch to EXISTING feature branch (NOT creating new one)
Step 20: Push to SAME branch (PR auto-updates)
key_constraints: ✅ REUSE existing feature branch from PR
❌ DO NOT create new fix/pull-request-... branches
✅ ALL fixes go to same feat/issue-#<N>-... branch
✅ Single PR per issue = clean merge history
❌ NEVER create separate PR for fixes
benefits: One feature branch per issue = simple merge
PR auto-updates with fixes = no tracking complexity
Clean commit history = easy to review changes
Avoids merge hell of multiple branches per issue
Clear traceability: one PR = all work for one issue

github_actions_automation.description: Automated PR merge to main via GitHub Actions

usage_policy.description: All Copilot CLI interactions must explicitly mention appropriate agent
cowork_examples: @orchestrator Read EXECUTION-PLAN-*.md and delegate to @developer agents for GitHub issues
@developer Implement issue #35 per execution plan; create branch, switch, implement, track files, git add [tracked files], commit, push, create PR
@reviewer Consolidate multiple PRs and verify integration testing; git switch to consolidation branch handles switching from any branch
@product-manager Create execution plan with multi-agent delegation strategy

git_commands.branch_switch_robust: git switch feat/issue-#<N>-<kebab-case> (handles from any branch)

specialist_agents.research_source: Industry best practice (Cloudflare 7-specialist pattern, academic research 2025-2026)
specialist_agents.description: Role-specific agents with focused prompts reduce noise and improve quality
specialist_agents.pattern: Coordinator deduplicates findings from 6+ specialists, posts single consolidated review

specialists.name: Implementer
specialists.responsibility: Generate syntactically valid code from specification
specialists.role_constraint: DO NOT worry about security/performance/docs—other specialists handle that

specialists.name: Security Reviewer
specialists.severity_mapping: Only true blockers marked as blocker; most findings minor-major

specialists.name: Performance Reviewer
specialists.responsibility: Detect algorithmic inefficiencies and resource issues
specialists.note: Deferred to non-blocking phase (not blocker unless catastrophic)

specialists.name: Test Writer
specialists.success_criteria: Coverage > 80%, assertions specific, edge cases covered

specialists.name: Architecture Reviewer
specialists.responsibility: Validate against design patterns and constraints
specialists.pattern: Reads DESIGN.md as source of truth

specialists.name: Documentation Agent
specialists.note: Runs after implementation passes all reviews

coordinator_pattern.description: Synthesizes 6+ specialist outputs into single consolidated review
responsibilities: Deduplication (same issue flagged by multiple agents)
Single structured comment (no noise, clear priorities)
coordinator_pattern.algorithm: Keep highest-severity per location, merge evidence, judge actual impact

state_management.research_source: Multi-agent AI systems research (token efficiency, state synchronization)
state_management.description: Structured state objects (200-500 tokens) instead of full context passing
state_management.pattern: SharedState → AgentContext → minimal relevant fields only

token_efficiency.naive_approach: Full conversation history = 5,000-20,000 tokens per agent ❌
token_efficiency.best_practice: Structured state object = 200-500 tokens per agent ✅
token_efficiency.savings: 20x token reduction, faster execution, better cache hits

event_driven_synchronization.description: Immutable event log prevents stale assumptions between parallel agents
event_driven_synchronization.pattern: Every agent action logged → state updates based on log → agents always see latest
event_driven_synchronization.benefit: No stale context; audit trail; resumable from last event

spec_driven_development.research_source: Spec-Driven Development 2025, GitHub Spec Kit, Intent.ai
spec_driven_development.description: Shared specification as single source of truth reduces agent drift
spec_driven_development.pattern: Specification evolves → agents reference spec → spec tracks progress
benefits: Explicit handoffs when agent needs work outside scope
spec_driven_development.implementation: Update execution plan with progress log; agents read spec before action

conflict_resolution.research_source: Parallel code change patterns, git merge best practices
conflict_resolution.strategy: File ownership + sequential merging + spec-based resolution

upfront_file_ownership.pattern: Directory-level ownership matrix (backend agent owns api/, frontend owns components/)
upfront_file_ownership.validation: Before parallel dispatch, verify no two agents touch same file

sequential_merging.pattern: Merge 1st agent → rebase others on updated base → merge sequentially
sequential_merging.benefit: Each merge only conflicts with directly preceding changes
sequential_merging.reason: Preserves intent; easier to resolve N conflicts than N² conflicts

ai_conflict_resolution.simple_conflicts: AI can resolve (two agents both added list entries)
ai_conflict_resolution.complex_conflicts: Orchestrator applies spec intent to resolve
ai_conflict_resolution.unresolvable: Escalate to human review with full context

preconditions: CRITICAL: Zero blocking dependencies between tasks
Different types of work (code, tests, docs)
Each agent gets isolated context window

error_recovery.research_source: Multi-agent failure recovery, self-healing agent pattern, FAILURE.md
error_recovery.description: Classify errors, map to recovery strategies, retry with exponential backoff
error_recovery.retry_logic: Attempt up to 3x with exponential backoff; escalate after 3 failures
error_recovery.memory_system: Track success patterns, failure patterns, learn from past attempts
error_recovery.self_validation: Agent validates outputs against explicit success criteria before returning

a2a_protocol.name: Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol
a2a_protocol.purpose: Interoperability between AI agents across frameworks and vendors
features: Security via API keys, OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect
a2a_protocol.when_to_use: Multi-vendor agent coordination (Claude Code + GitHub Copilot + custom)

metadata.feature: Multi-agent orchestration with execution plans and parallel execution best practices
research_applied: Token-efficient state management (200-500 tokens per agent)
key_improvements: Specialist agent roles with focused prompts
Error recovery with retry and escalation
